title: Vincetoxicum affine
taxonomy: Vincetoxicum affine is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is also known as a type of milkweed or swallowwort, with the specific epithet 'affine' indicating its close relationship to other species within the Vincetoxicum genus. Vincetoxicum affine is native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been classified as a perennial herb with showy, white or pink flowers. It belongs to the Apocynaceae family, which comprises a large group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbanes or oleander family.

side_effects: Vincetoxicum affine is known to cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to its toxic compounds. intro: Vincetoxicum affine, also known as Chinese swallowwort or snake root, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anticancer properties, as well as cardiovascular and neuroprotective effects. The bioactive constituents of Vincetoxicum affine include diterpenoids, flavonoids, and saponins, which have been found to contribute to its medicinal properties.
